title: Triticum heldreichii
taxonomy: Triticum heldreichii is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Triticum, which includes various wheat species. This genus is further categorized under the tribe Triticeae, a group of grasses that also includes barley and rye. The species Triticum heldreichii is native to the Balkan Peninsula and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation. It is a hexaploid species, meaning it has six sets of chromosomes, like many other wheat species.

intro: Triticum heldreichii, also known as Heldreich's wheat or wild emmer, is a rare and ancient species of wheat native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Triticum heldreichii include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and phytosterols, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
